bullets.text: Q1 2013 combined ratio was 92.4, well inside the 96 target, with net income growth of 20%, though results included ~$65 million of unfavorable prior accident year development.
Written premium grew 7% and earned premium grew 8% year-over-year in Q1, driven primarily by higher average rates rather than unit growth.

Unfavorable prior-year development of ~$65 million stemmed from underestimated frequency and severity of late-emerging bodily injury and property damage claims in Agency Personal Lines and Commercial Auto.
Unit growth was weak at 1–2% for both Personal Lines and Commercial Auto, though new application trends began improving toward quarter-end as rate competitiveness stabilized.
Atypically early hail storms in Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Georgia damaged ~16,000 insured vehicles, making catastrophe losses an unexpected Q1 factor.
Management expressed comfort that auto rate levels were close to target; policy life expectancy decline was expected to reverse as customers renewed within the same rate plan.

Agency conversion rate was lower than prior year despite comparable quote volumes; Direct new applications crossed from below to above prior-year levels during the quarter aided by Internet and mobile demand.
Mobile acquisition capabilities were expanded to cover nearly all car-and-driver combinations, with service enhancements planned; Snapshot device was piloted with thousands of agents.
Progressive Home Advantage marketing was advanced through the ASI relationship; two new co-located Service Centers opened in Maryland and Pennsylvania, with seven more planned by year-end.

A new Snapshot advertising campaign launched post-quarter, shifting messaging from 'you could save' to emphasizing that non-participants may subsidize poor drivers, targeting consumer fairness sentiment.
